citation: Miller et al., FlowMM: Generating Materials with Riemannian Flow Matching, 2024
use: Adopt a true Dirichlet simplex base, exact simplex retraction/tangent projection, no-drift torus quotient, and endpoint type decoder objective; do not restore FlowMM as a runtime dependency.

citation: Gat et al., Discrete Flow Matching, 2024
use: Supports a posterior/endpoint-prediction view of discrete generation; A5 implements the simpler FlowMM-style simplex endpoint objective, not a continuous-time Markov sampler.

citation: Lipman et al., Flow Matching for Generative Modeling, 2023; Pooladian et al., Multisample Flow Matching, 2023
use: Supports replacing arbitrary independent source-target pairing by an explicit lower-cost coupling. A5 uses training-only periodic Hungarian coupling, never a target structure at sampling.
